settings.text: We work with businesses like yours.

The rhythms of a creative practice look nothing like a construction firm's, and a nonprofit's compliance calendar is its own world. Our engagements are shaped around the industry, not forced into a template.

settings.title: Professional services
settings.description: Consultants, agencies, design studios, and advisory firms. Pass-through structures, owner compensation, and project-based revenue recognition.

settings.title: E-commerce & retail
settings.description: Shopify and marketplace sellers, multi-channel inventory, sales tax in every state, and margin analysis that tells you what's actually working.

settings.title: Real estate & property
settings.description: Landlords, investors, and short-term rental operators. Depreciation strategy, cost segregation, 1031 exchanges, and schedule-E returns done right.

settings.title: Nonprofits & foundations
settings.description: Form 990, restricted-fund tracking, board-ready financial statements, and the compliance calendar that keeps your exempt status clean.

settings.title: Self-employed & consultants
settings.description: Freelancers, independent contractors, and one-person firms. Quarterly estimates, Schedule C, self-employed retirement contributions, home-office deductions.

settings.title: Medical & dental practices
settings.description: Solo and group practices, equipment depreciation, cost accounting by provider, and payroll that handles both W-2 staff and contract clinicians.

settings.title: The work changes with the industry.
settings.description: Every engagement starts with understanding how your business actually makes money — because that's what shapes the books, the tax posture, and the conversations we have each quarter.

settings.title: Industry-specific chart of accounts
settings.description: A dental practice's books look different from an agency's. We structure accounts around how your industry actually reads its numbers.

settings.title: Tax strategy by sector
settings.description: Cost segregation for real estate. QBI planning for pass-throughs. 990 strategy for nonprofits. We know where the levers are in your industry.

settings.title: Compliance calendars that fit
settings.description: Your deadlines — grants, 1099s, sales tax, franchise tax, board reports — tracked and filed without you having to remember any of them.

settings.title: Benchmarks that mean something
settings.description: We compare your margins, payroll ratios, and overhead against peers in your industry — not generic small-business averages.

settings.quote: They actually understand restaurants. Our last accountant treated us like a widget business — Ledgerworks knew the margin math on day one.
settings.name: Alex R.
settings.title: Restaurant owner

settings.quote: As a freelance designer I was filing quarterly estimates with spreadsheets and a lot of guessing. That ended the week they took over.
settings.name: Priya S.
settings.title: Freelance designer

settings.quote: Cost segregation on two properties paid for three years of their fee in the first year. Wish I'd found them sooner.
settings.name: Daniel K.
settings.title: Real estate investor

settings.text: If you're a small business with a CPA-sized problem, chances are we've seen something close.
